audit_text: `session.edit` missing from registry. `MENU_ACTION_SESSION_EDIT` is defined in `config.ts`, labeled, and wired in `SessionRow.svelte` (opens `SessionEdit` modal), but absent from `SESSION_ACTIONS` array in `context-menu/registry.ts`. Menu is driven by that array — action never renders. Fix: add `{ id: MENU_ACTION_SESSION_EDIT, section: MENU_SECTION_EDIT }` above `session.rename` in `SESSION_ACTIONS`.
spec_anchor: docs/behavior/context-menus.md §"Per-target action lists" → "Session row (sidebar) and conversation header" — `session.edit | Opens the SessionEdit modal (Title, Description, Budget, Tags, Instructions).`
verdict_evidence: registry.ts SESSION_ACTIONS at lines 142-161 contains MENU_ACTION_SESSION_OPEN_IN_NEW_TAB (143), MENU_ACTION_SESSION_RENAME (144), MENU_ACTION_SESSION_EDIT_TAGS (145), MENU_ACTION_SESSION_CHANGE_MODEL (146), … but NOT MENU_ACTION_SESSION_EDIT. The constant is imported and bound elsewhere: config.ts:2781 defines `"session.edit"`, config.ts:2886 labels it `"Edit session…"`, SessionRow.svelte:316 maps the id to `void openEditModal()`. The menu primitive iterates SESSION_ACTIONS to render rows, so the wired handler is unreachable. No commits since 2026-05-07 touch registry.ts.
acceptance_criteria: `MENU_ACTION_SESSION_EDIT` is imported in registry.ts.

SESSION_ACTIONS contains `{ id: MENU_ACTION_SESSION_EDIT, section: MENU_SECTION_EDIT }` immediately above the `MENU_ACTION_SESSION_RENAME` entry (i.e. as the first MENU_SECTION_EDIT entry, matching the doc's row order: Edit session… → Rename… → Edit tags… → Change model for continuation).

Right-clicking a sidebar session row renders the `Edit session…` row in the edit section of the context menu, and clicking it opens the SessionEdit modal (Title, Description, Budget, Tags, Instructions).
executor_brief: Add `MENU_ACTION_SESSION_EDIT` to the imports in `frontend/src/lib/context-menu/registry.ts` and insert `{ id: MENU_ACTION_SESSION_EDIT, section: MENU_SECTION_EDIT }` as the first entry in `SESSION_ACTIONS` (above the existing `MENU_ACTION_SESSION_RENAME` row). Constant + label + handler already exist (`config.ts:2781,2886`, `SessionRow.svelte:316`); the only missing piece is the registry entry that drives rendering. Two-line change.

audit_text: Ctrl+K binding marked `global:true` instead of `displayOnly:true`. Inline comment and behavior doc both say it is wired by the sidebar search component. Not a visible bug today (no global handler = no-op) but it is in the runtime registry map and will fire inside the composer if a global handler is ever registered. Fix: replace `global:true` with `global:false + displayOnly:true`. Joint action item with #5 (sidebar search) — same Ctrl+K chord.
spec_anchor: docs/behavior/keyboard-shortcuts.md §"Command palette" — "`Ctrl+K` is wired by the sidebar search component itself (the focus target is colocated with the input); the cheat sheet lists it for discoverability." Conflict-resolution §"Native browser binding overlap" likewise classifies Ctrl+K as `displayOnly`.
code_paths_checked: frontend/src/lib/keyboard/dispatch.ts:24-25 (dispatcher comment confirming displayOnly entries are NOT inserted into the runtime map)
verdict_evidence: bindings.ts:184-189 declares the chord `{ code: "KeyK", ctrl: true, … }` for KEYBINDING_ACTION_FOCUS_SIDEBAR_SEARCH with `global: true` and no `displayOnly` field. The KeybindingSpec interface (lines 62-66) explicitly defines the displayOnly pattern: "the chord is wired by a colocated component (e.g. ``Ctrl+K`` lives on the sidebar search). The cheat sheet shows it for discoverability; the global dispatcher does NOT consume it." Behavior doc keyboard-shortcuts.md states the same. Today the dispatcher inserts Ctrl+K into the global map; if a future handler is registered there, it pre-empts the colocated sidebar-search owner and fires inside the composer. No commits since 2026-05-07 touch bindings.ts.
acceptance_criteria: The KEYBINDING_ACTION_FOCUS_SIDEBAR_SEARCH spec in `bindings.ts` has `global: false` and `displayOnly: true`.

The dispatcher (`frontend/src/lib/keyboard/dispatch.ts`) does NOT insert Ctrl+K into its runtime chord-handler map — verified by an existing or new unit test asserting `displayOnly` chords are skipped.
The cheat-sheet modal still renders the Ctrl+K row in the Command palette section (display-only listing remains).
Sidebar search continues to focus on Ctrl+K via the colocated handler in the sidebar search component.
executor_brief: In `frontend/src/lib/keyboard/bindings.ts:184-189`, change the KEYBINDING_ACTION_FOCUS_SIDEBAR_SEARCH spec from `global: true` to `global: false, displayOnly: true`. The KeybindingSpec interface already defines `displayOnly` (lines 62-66) and the dispatcher (`dispatch.ts:24-25`) already excludes displayOnly entries from the runtime map. Add or extend a unit test in `frontend/src/lib/keyboard/__tests__/dispatch.test.ts` confirming Ctrl+K is not in the dispatcher map. Joint with audit-item #5 (sidebar search) — same chord.

audit_text: Context menu cheat-sheet missing Home/End. Behavior doc lists them, `ContextMenu.svelte` handles them (lines 387/394), but `nonRegistryBindings.ts` `context_menu` section omits them.
spec_anchor: docs/behavior/context-menus.md §"Common behavior across every menu" line 16 — "Up / Down navigate; Home jumps to the first enabled action; End jumps to the last enabled action; Enter activates; Right opens a submenu; Esc closes."
code_paths_checked: frontend/src/lib/context-menu/ContextMenu.svelte:387-406 (Home/End key handlers present and call preventDefault + syncFocus)
verdict_evidence: nonRegistryBindings.ts:44-53 defines the cheat-sheet `context_menu` section with rows for ↑/↓, Enter, →, Esc, and A–Z mnemonic only. ContextMenu.svelte:387 handles `event.key === "Home"` (jumps to first enabled action via `flatActions.findIndex((a) => isActionEnabled(a))`), and ContextMenu.svelte:394 handles `event.key === "End"` (walks backwards to last enabled action). Both branches call `event.preventDefault()` and `syncFocus(highlightIndex)`. Behavior doc context-menus.md line 16 documents Home/End in the common-behavior block, so the cheat-sheet is missing two documented bindings. No commits since 2026-05-07 touch nonRegistryBindings.ts.
acceptance_criteria: The `context_menu` section in `nonRegistryBindings.ts` includes `{ keys: ["Home"], label: "Jump to first enabled action" }` and `{ keys: ["End"], label: "Jump to last enabled action" }`.
The cheat-sheet modal renders both rows under the Context menu section (verifiable via the existing CheatSheet test suite — extend `__tests__/CheatSheet.test.ts` to assert the two rows appear).
Row order in the section reflects keyboard-navigation grouping: arrow / Home / End / Enter / → / Esc / mnemonic.
executor_brief: In `frontend/src/lib/keyboard/nonRegistryBindings.ts`, add Home and End rows to the `context_menu` section's `bindings` array (between the existing arrow-navigation row and the Enter row). Wording matches the behavior doc: "Jump to first enabled action" / "Jump to last enabled action". Update `__tests__/CheatSheet.test.ts` to assert both rows render. No code change needed in ContextMenu.svelte — handlers already exist at lines 387 and 394.

audit_text: `session.merge_into` and `session.export_json` are fully wired (registry, `SESSION_ACTIONS`, handlers in `SessionRow.svelte`, `SessionPickerModal.svelte` for merge) but neither appears in `docs/behavior/context-menus.md`. Action IDs are public/stable — they need to be documented.
spec_anchor: docs/behavior/context-menus.md §"Per-target action lists" → "Session row (sidebar) and conversation header" — table currently lists open_in_new_tab, edit, rename, edit_tags, change_model, duplicate, save_as_template, fork.from_last_message, pin/unpin/archive/reopen, copy_id/copy_title/copy_share_link, delete, open_in_terminal — but NOT merge_into or export_json.
code_paths_checked: docs/behavior/context-menus.md (Session row table — both action IDs absent; full grep for `merge_into` / `export_json` returns zero matches)
verdict_evidence: `grep -n 'session\.merge_into\|session\.export_json\|merge_into\|export_json' docs/behavior/context-menus.md` returns no matches. The action IDs are stable per the doc's preamble ("Action IDs (e.g. `session.copy_id`) are public — `~/.config/bearings/menus.toml` references them by ID to pin or hide entries."). Both are fully wired in HEAD: registry.ts SESSION_ACTIONS lines 150 (merge_into / organize section) and 158 (export_json / copy section); SessionRow.svelte handlers lines 379-381 (export) and 388-390 (merge); resolving commits 4ac748af (export, gap-cycle-03-003) and 7f053a57 (merge, gap-cycle-03-008). The doc was not updated alongside those commits.
acceptance_criteria: The Session row table in `docs/behavior/context-menus.md` §"Per-target action lists" gains a row `organize | Merge into… | session.merge_into | Opens session picker; reorgs the chosen target into this session via POST /api/sessions/{src}/reorg/merge.` (or wording consistent with the existing table style).
The same table gains a row `copy | Export session JSON | session.export_json | Downloads the session as JSON via GET /api/sessions/{id}/export.`

Row order respects the section ordering rule (primary → navigate → create → edit → view → copy → organize → destructive). merge_into goes in organize alongside pin/unpin/archive; export_json goes in copy.
Both action IDs are present in a future grep of the doc (catches regression).
Lychee link-check still passes (no broken anchors introduced).
executor_brief: Pure documentation update. In `docs/behavior/context-menus.md` §"Per-target action lists" → "Session row (sidebar) and conversation header" table, add two rows: `session.merge_into` (organize section, opens session picker, calls POST /api/sessions/{src}/reorg/merge per commit 7f053a57) and `session.export_json` (copy section, GET /api/sessions/{id}/export per commit 4ac748af). Match the table's existing wording style (concise Notes column). Part of cross-cutting work-stream CCW-1 (behavior-doc / release-notes correction pass).

audit_text: Theme persistence is localStorage-only; behavior doc says per-account server-synced. Tracked as Item 2.9 theme server-sync layer.
spec_anchor: docs/behavior/themes.md §"Persistence boundary" — "Per-account, server-synced. The user's chosen theme persists on the server and is read back when the same account opens Bearings on the same device next time." §"Failure modes" — "Server-sync write fails (network blip on the preferences PATCH). The local preview reverts to whatever the server still has on file; the user sees a 'couldn't save your theme — try again' toast."
code_paths_checked: frontend/src/lib/themes/persistence.ts:1-119 (entire module — exclusively localStorage; no fetch / API client / preferences PATCH)
TODO.md:164-190 (Item 2.9 — theme server-sync layer (deferred) — explicitly tracks this gap with rationale)
verdict_evidence: persistence.ts implements `loadStoredTheme()` (reads `window.localStorage.getItem(THEME_STORAGE_KEY)`), `saveStoredTheme()` (writes `window.localStorage.setItem(...)`), and `resolveBootTheme()` (falls back to OS color-scheme then persists to localStorage). No code path calls the preferences API. The behavior doc prescribes server-synced persistence with a network-failure toast. TODO.md:164 explicitly logs this as a deliberate v1 deferral ("v1 ships **localStorage-only** persistence"). Per-audit framing is "Known deferred" — the gap remains in HEAD but is intentionally scheduled out of v1 ship.
acceptance_criteria: Either: (a) the v1 spec in `docs/behavior/themes.md` §"Persistence boundary" is amended to declare per-device localStorage as the v1 contract (matching reality, citing TODO.md Item 2.9 as the v1.x follow-up), OR (b) `persistence.ts` gains a `persistThemeToServer(theme)` call wired to the preferences PATCH endpoint, with a network-failure path that shows the documented "couldn't save your theme — try again" toast and reverts the local preview to the previously-saved value.
If (b): localStorage remains the synchronous boot-time read so the no-flash guarantee in §"What gets re-themed live" still holds. Server-sync runs after the synchronous local-paint.
If (b): an integration test asserts a 5xx on the preferences PATCH triggers the toast and the runtime store reverts to the prior value.
If (b) lands, TODO.md Item 2.9 is struck (with the resolving commit hash cited per the global TODO.md discipline).
Behavior doc and runtime agree (no spec-vs-reality drift remains).
executor_brief: Two routes per the audit ("already deferred"): the cheap close is doc-side — amend `docs/behavior/themes.md` §"Persistence boundary" to match reality (localStorage-only in v1, server-sync post-v1) and cite TODO.md Item 2.9. The full close is the deferred Item 2.9 itself: build the preferences route + DB column for theme, extend `persistence.ts` with a server-PATCH layer behind the existing saveTheme/loadTheme shape (localStorage stays as the boot-time synchronous read for the no-flash guarantee), wire the network-failure toast. Decision-discipline note: this is explicitly logged as a v1.x follow-up; if the v1 ship line is the gate, pick (a) doc amendment to align v1 contract with shipped behavior.
